messages.content: Find where the sidebar renders the account pill and check for a stray CSS rule causing double borders.

## Summary

Found it. The account pill was picking up two conflicting border rules:

- `.account-row` set a `1px solid` border for the whole row
- `.account-pill` also set its own border, which combined to look like a double outline

### Fix

- Removed the border from `.account-row`
- Kept the border on `.account-pill` only

Quick checklist:

- [x] Reproduced the double border in the sidebar
- [x] Located both conflicting rules
- [x] Removed the redundant one
- [ ] Visual re-check after the fix

The collision came from a leftover rule that predates the pill refactor — `git blame` points at a commit from before the account-switcher redesign. Run `npm run dev` and check `--- .sidebar .account-row ---` styling isn't reintroduced elsewhere.

---

Let me know if you'd like the same cleanup applied to the compact density variant.

input.description: Check recent history for the border rule

toolCalls.result:
a9a5983 fix(sidebar): flatten account row and fix .account-row style collision
ec20555 fix(sidebar): account pill shows name only, detail moves to tooltip
4b1f0aa feat(sidebar): initial account switcher UI

input.description: Verify the CSS change didn't break the build
